ocr_model_repair.title: CarbonPaper OCR model needs repair
ocr_model_repair.body: Screenshots will still be saved, but OCR text is unavailable until the model is repaired.

language.label: Language
language.description: Select display language for the interface.

telemetry.label: Allow sending telemetry diagnostic data
telemetry.description: Allow program to upload error logs that do not contain privacy data to the telemetry server.

powerSaving.label: Power Saving Mode
powerSaving.description: Automatically pause Python sub-service when on battery power, and resume when plugged in.

gameMode.label: Game Mode
gameMode.description: When enabled will disable DML and screenshots on game launch for better game performance.
gameMode.requires_dml: Requires DirectML acceleration to be enabled first
gameMode.permanent: DirectML disabled (too many switches, will restore on restart)
gameMode.fullscreen_paused: Active: Non-browser fullscreen app detected, capture paused

resourcePolicy.label: Resource Usage
resourcePolicy.description: Choose how aggressively CarbonPaper should protect battery life and foreground performance.

complete.label: Complete
complete.description: Keep capture and analysis running whenever possible. Best for a complete history, but may affect games and battery life.

balanced.label: Balanced
balanced.description: Pause heavier background services on battery power, then resume when plugged in.

performance.label: Performance
performance.description: Default. Protect battery life and reduce load during games or fullscreen apps.

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: Tune battery saving and game protection separately.

startHidden.label: Start with Hidden Window (Lightweight Mode)
startHidden.description: Start without showing the UI, run background services only, accessible via tray menu

autoSwitch.label: Auto-switch to Lightweight Mode
autoSwitch.description: Automatically switch to lightweight mode after window closes to save memory

switchNow.label: Switch to Lightweight Mode Now
switchNow.description: Close UI and free memory, can reopen via tray menu

windowBehavior.label: Window and Background
windowBehavior.description: Control whether CarbonPaper shows a window on startup and keeps running in the background after closing.

showWindow.label: Show window
showWindow.description: Open the main window when CarbonPaper starts.

background.label: Background
background.description: Start hidden in the tray and run in the background.

current.label: Just hide window
current.description: Hide the UI to the tray and keep current background services as they are.

background.label: Run in background
background.description: Hide the UI immediately, then switch to background running after the delay.

switchNow.label: Run in background now
switchNow.description: Hide the main window immediately and switch to background running.

cardClickBehavior.label: Card Click Default Behavior
cardClickBehavior.description: Configure the default action when clicking on snapshot cards under different functional views.

monitor.title: Monitor Service
monitor.tooltip: The Python service handles screenshot capture, OCR text recognition, and image vectorization. When stopped, screenshots and natural language search are unavailable, but existing OCR search still works.
monitor.description: Control the background screenshot & OCR service.
monitor.power_saving_blocked: Power saving mode is active. Connect AC power to start the service.

autoStart.label: Auto-start Subservice
autoStart.description: When enabled the app will attempt to launch the Python subservice on startup.
autoStart.tooltip: Automatically start screenshot/OCR background on app launch

autoLaunch.label: Auto Launch at Startup
autoLaunch.description: Writes to HKLM; administrative confirmation may be required.
autoLaunch.enabledMessage: Enabled: CarbonPaper will start on system startup
autoLaunch.disabledMessage: Disabled: Will not start with system

errors.manualRestartRecovery: ; recovery policy: manual restart, stale IPC state cleaned

protection.title: Protected by Windows CNG
protection.description: Your snapshot data is encrypted using credentials managed by Windows CNG. Only authorized access can decrypt it.

session.title: Auto-lock timeout
session.description: Set how long of inactivity before automatic lock requiring re-authentication.

advanced.warning: These settings may affect monitor service performance and system stability. Adjust carefully.

terms.directml: DirectML is Microsoft's GPU inference interface. It can accelerate OCR on compatible GPUs, but may compete with games or graphics-heavy apps.
terms.onnx: ONNX is a portable model format/runtime. It usually uses less memory than PyTorch and starts faster.
terms.pacmap_hdbscan: PaCMAP reduces high-dimensional text vectors into a shape that is easier to cluster; HDBSCAN then groups dense regions without needing a fixed cluster count.
terms.minilm: MiniLM-L12 is the compact language model used to encode OCR text before task clustering.

cpu.title: Python subprocess CPU limits
cpu.label: Limit CPU usage
cpu.description: Limit CPU resources used for OCR and embeddings to avoid impacting system performance
cpu.changed_notice: CPU limit changes take effect when the monitor service restarts

ocr.title: OCR processing
ocr.single_flight_desc: OCR processes RGB capture frames before JPEG encoding. To cap memory use, full RGB frames are not queued while OCR is running.
ocr.raw_info: JPEG is used only for encrypted storage and Python post-processing. It is never OCR input or an OCR failure fallback.
ocr.pause_label: Pause captures when OCR queue has work
ocr.pause_desc: When enabled, pauses captures while there are unprocessed OCR tasks to reduce resource usage
ocr.queue_limit_desc: Pause captures until queue is drained when above this size
ocr.timeout_desc: Set the timeout for OCR tasks. Cold starts always allow 180 seconds.
ocr.info: OCR queue settings take effect immediately without restarting the monitor service

rust_ocr.title: OCR engine
rust_ocr.raw_rgb_desc: The isolated Rust ML process reads RGB capture frames directly, without JPEG encoding or Python OCR fallback.
rust_ocr.rust_desc: Runs OCR directly on RGB capture frames in an isolated Rust ML process.
rust_ocr.python_desc: Python OCR is no longer used as the screenshot OCR fallback.
rust_ocr.dml_beta_desc: Temporary experimental switch, off by default. It will be removed in favor of the unified DirectML setting.
rust_ocr.model_bundled: PP-OCRv5 Mobile is bundled with CarbonPaper
rust_ocr.model_repaired: PP-OCRv5 Mobile was installed by online repair
rust_ocr.model_damaged: PP-OCRv5 Mobile installation resources are missing or corrupt

dml.title: OCR inference acceleration
dml.label: Use DirectML for inference
dml.description: When enabled, uses GPU for OCR inference acceleration; requires DirectX 12 compatible GPU
dml.changed_notice: DirectML changes take effect when the monitor service restarts
dml.notice: Note: Running DirectML on the primary GPU may impact gaming performance.

clustering.title: Task Clustering
clustering.interval_desc: Set the time interval for automatic background task clustering
clustering.allow_full_low_memory_label: Allow full auto-clustering under low memory
clustering.allow_full_low_memory_desc: Off by default. When off, automatic clustering uses batched degraded mode for 20k+ snapshots under low memory; when on, it still attempts full PaCMAP + HDBSCAN and may page heavily.
clustering.info: Task clustering automatically groups similar activities into long-term tasks. Uses MiniLM-L12 model for text encoding; the model is automatically unloaded after clustering to free memory.

network.title: Network Control
network.label: Enable Network Features
network.description: When disabled, all external network requests will be blocked, including update checks and model downloads.

onnx.title: ONNX Inference (Experimental)
onnx.label: Use ONNX model inference
onnx.description: When enabled, uses ONNX models instead of PyTorch models. This significantly reduces memory usage (~250-400MB) and speeds up startup and inference.
onnx.notice: Note: Enabling this option will automatically download the required ONNX model files when the settings dialog is closed.
onnx.changed_notice: ONNX settings will take effect when the monitor service restarts

vacuum.title: Database maintenance
vacuum.label: Run database optimization manually
vacuum.description: VACUUM can reclaim database space and reorganize storage. It may take a few seconds to several minutes.
vacuum.already_running: A database optimization task is already running. Please wait.

storageManagement.title: Storage Management
storageManagement.description: Manage snapshot storage space and data retention policy

storagePath.label: Storage Location
storagePath.description: Select and migrate the directory used for snapshots and database
storagePath.migrateQuestion: Migrate existing data files to the new directory?

storageLimit.label: Snapshot storage limit
storageLimit.description: Old snapshots will be deleted automatically when exceeded

retention.label: Snapshot retention period
retention.description: Snapshots older than this period will be pruned

warning.title: Warning
warning.message: Current settings allow unlimited storage and permanent retention; snapshots may consume large disk space. Consider setting reasonable limits or retention.

migration.background_tip: You can safely close this window. The upgrade will continue in the background, and search results will gradually populate.
migration.search_unavailable_desc: The database is undergoing a security upgrade and the search index is being rebuilt. During this time, search results may be incomplete or temporarily unavailable. Normal service will resume automatically once the upgrade completes.

vacuum.running: Running VACUUM database optimization. Please wait...
vacuum.tip: The database is temporarily locked during optimization. This window cannot be closed and will resume automatically when complete.

deleteConfirm.title: Confirm deletion
deleteConfirm.messageSelected: Delete the selected {{count}} screenshots? They will be hidden immediately, and cleanup will run in the background.
deleteConfirm.messageMonth: Delete screenshots of {{processName}} in {{month}}? They will be hidden immediately, and cleanup will run in the background.
deleteConfirm.messageProcess: Delete all screenshots of {{processName}}? They will be hidden immediately, and cleanup will run in the background.

processDetails.title: Storage by Process
processDetails.description: View process share, monthly thumbnails, then select and delete
processDetails.empty: No screenshots available for this process

processes.label: Ignore by process name
processes.hint: Matches by executable name, automatically lowercased.

ignoreProtected.label: Do not capture protected windows
ignoreProtected.description: When disabled, attempts to capture windows that have screen-protected attributes.

quickDelete.title: Quick Delete Records
quickDelete.description: Quickly delete all screenshots and OCR records within the specified range. This action cannot be undone.

extension.title: Browser Extension

enhance.label: Extension Enhancement
enhance.description: Install the CarbonPaper browser extension to capture viewport screenshots with page metadata (URL, title, visible links). Works with any Chromium-based browser.

extension.success: Extension installed successfully. Please enable the unpacked extension in your browser.
extension.help: After clicking the button, load the unpacked extension from the path shown in your browser's extension settings page.

tutorial.title: How to install
tutorial.step1: Click the install button above. "Chrome / Chromium browsers" also covers other Chromium-based browsers (360, Cent, Brave, ...).
tutorial.step2: Open the browser extensions page and enable "Developer mode".
tutorial.step3: Click "Load unpacked" and select the extension folder shown after installation.
tutorial.step4: The CarbonPaper extension icon will appear in your browser toolbar, and the browser shows up under "Connected browsers".

ai_embedding.title: AI Embedding
ai_embedding.description: Expose a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) HTTP server so that AI assistants can query your snapshot data, OCR text, and task clusters.
ai_embedding.mcp_description: When enabled, starts a local MCP service that lets AI tools query snapshot data, including screenshot metadata, OCR text, and task cluster information. Be mindful of privacy and only authorize AI tools you trust.

privacy_warning.title: Privacy Warning
privacy_warning.message:
Enabling the MCP server will allow any application on this machine that knows the token to access your snapshot data, including screenshots, OCR text, and task information.

The server binds to localhost only and is not accessible from external networks, but any local process with the token can read your data.

Please make sure you understand the risks before enabling.
privacy_warning.confirm_prompt: Type the following text to confirm:
privacy_warning.confirm_text: I have read the warning and I accept the potential exposure risk

token.title: Access Token
token.description: The token is used for API authentication. It is only shown once when generated.
token.show_once_warning: This token will only be shown once. Please copy and save it now. You will not be able to view it again.
token.reset_confirm_message: The current token will be permanently invalidated. All clients using the old token will lose access. A new token will be generated.

status.pending_auth: Enabled. The MCP server will start automatically after Windows Hello authentication.

agent_setup.title: Agent Setup
agent_setup.description: Copy setup instructions so an Agent can fetch the Skill and connect to the local MCP server.
agent_setup.prompt:
Configure CarbonPaper MCP access.

1. Fetch the Skill from the {{skillName}} directory in GitHub repository {{repo}}, then install it into your Agent/Skill environment.
2. Use that Skill for CarbonPaper screenshot history, OCR, task cluster, and smart-cluster summary workflows.
3. MCP endpoint: {{endpoint}}, using Streamable HTTP/HTTP POST.
4. Authorization header: Authorization: Bearer <CarbonPaper token>. Do not ask me to paste the token into this long setup prompt; ask me to click Copy token in CarbonPaper and configure it separately in your MCP client.
5. If a tool returns AUTH_REQUIRED, tell me to unlock CarbonPaper with Windows Hello before retrying.
6. This MCP server may expose local screenshot history, OCR text, window titles, URLs, task clusters, and smart-cluster data. Read only the content needed for my explicit request.

content_filter.title: Content Filter
content_filter.description: Filter flagged content from MCP server responses based on compliance rules.

standard.title: Standard Protection
standard.description: Filter content that may trigger AI provider review, preventing your account from being flagged or data from being manually reviewed.

minimal.title: Minimal Filtering
minimal.description: Only filter content likely to cause API request rejection.

off.title: Off
off.description: All screenshot content may be sent to your AI provider.

custom.title: Custom
custom.description: Category selection has been manually customized.

reject.title: Reject Entire Snapshot
reject.description: Block the entire snapshot from being returned when sensitive content is detected.

remove_paragraph.title: Remove Sensitive Paragraphs
remove_paragraph.description: Remove OCR text entries that contain sensitive words; the rest of the snapshot is still returned.

mask.title: Mask Sensitive Words
mask.description: Replace sensitive words with █ characters; the snapshot and surrounding text are still returned.

categories.cat_05: Content That May Trigger Safety Review

pii.title: PII Detection
pii.description: Detect and filter personal identifiable information (names, phone numbers, ID cards, etc.) from MCP responses using NLP models.
pii.model_note: Active model follows your language setting

backup.export_hint: This will pack your database and snapshots into an encrypted ZIP. The password is required to decrypt the backup on another computer. PLEASE KEEP IT SAFE.
backup.import_hint: Importing will replace your current data with the contents of the backup. This action cannot be undone.
backup.processing_hint: Please do not close the application.
backup.import_success_hint: Data has been imported. Please restart the application to apply changes.

featureMode.label: Feature Level
featureMode.description: Choose how much semantic functionality CarbonPaper should enable. Higher levels provide more automation and may use more background resources.

minimal.label: Minimal
minimal.description: Keep basic screenshots and OCR search without semantic classification or clustering.

basic.label: Basic
basic.description: Enable content classification for filtering, statistics, and later organization.

organized.label: Organized
organized.description: Enable content classification and task clustering to organize long-running activity.

smart.label: Smart
smart.description: Enable all semantic features, including automatic archive by description.

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: The current feature combination came from individual settings or an older configuration.

clustering.label: Task Clustering
clustering.description: Group similar activities into long-term tasks using MiniLM model
clustering.interval_desc: Set the interval for background auto clustering

classification.label: Content Classification
classification.description: Automatically classify screenshot content using BGE model

smartCluster.label: Smart Clusters (auto-archive by description)
smartCluster.description: Auto-group related screenshots by natural-language description
smartCluster.clusteringDisabledWarning: Task Clustering is disabled. Smart Clusters can still run during rescans, but without automatic text vectorization at screenshot capture time, candidate retrieval must encode text on demand, which may significantly reduce processing performance and increase resource usage.

indexHealth.label: Index Health
indexHealth.description: Current status of screenshots, OCR, vector indexes, and background retry queues

updateModal.title: New Version Released
updateModal.criticalNotice: This update includes important security fixes or feature improvements. To ensure your data safety and the best experience, needs update as soon as possible.

save_filters.synced: Saved and synced to monitor service
save_filters.saved_local_not_running: Saved locally; monitor service not running, will sync when started
save_filters.saved_local_unsupported: Saved locally, but current monitor process does not support filter commands. Please restart monitor service

nl.disabled_hint: Requires Python service to be running

ocr.title: OCR Keyword Search
ocr.description: Search by matching text recognized in screenshots

nl.title: Natural Language Search
nl.description: Describe the image content, scene or objects for semantic fuzzy search

task.modelDownloadRunningDesc: Downloading required model files in background, progress: {{progress}}%.

sidebar.empty: Select an event from the timeline to view details.

activityContext.title: Activity Context
activityContext.deleteActivityConfirm: This removes the activity and all of its assignments, but keeps every snapshot. Continue?
activityContext.removeSnapshotConfirm: This only removes this snapshot from the current activity. The snapshot itself is kept. Continue?

extensionSetup.title: Browser Extension
extensionSetup.subtitle: Capture browser screenshots with rich metadata
extensionSetup.description: The CarbonPaper browser extension captures viewport screenshots along with page metadata such as URL, title, and visible links. Select the browsers you use to install the extension.
extensionSetup.success_message: Extension installed successfully. Enable Developer mode in your browser and load the unpacked extension from the path below.

clusteringSetup.title: Organize Activity History
clusteringSetup.subtitle: Automatically group existing snapshots into tasks
clusteringSetup.description: This version can automatically cluster your accumulated screenshots into "Tasks" based on their content, helping you review and organize your work history by topic.
clusteringSetup.delay_hint: After confirming, clustering will start automatically in the background after a 1-minute delay (to allow the embedding model to load). You can continue using the app normally.
clusteringSetup.info_note: You can also skip this step and manually run clustering later from Settings > Features > Task Clustering.

nlCluster.errorMinPositives: At least {{count}} positive examples are required before saving
nlCluster.errorCalculateThreshold: Could not calculate a threshold from positive scores. Search again, then mark examples.
nlCluster.calibratePlaceholder: Describe what you want to auto-archive, for example "research about California mountain ranges"
nlCluster.explorePlaceholder: Try "code and documents about neural network training"
nlCluster.onnxVariantTooltip: ONNX variant. Switching reloads the model.
nlCluster.calibrationTip1: Tip: click a card to open it in preview. Your marks are kept when you return.
nlCluster.calibrationTip2: At least {{count}} positive examples are required before saving.
nlCluster.loadingSearching: Encoding the query and matching snapshots...
nlCluster.calibrateInstruction: Enter a description and click Preview candidates to list the most relevant snapshots for marking
nlCluster.exploreInstruction: Enter a natural-language description to retrieve the most similar snapshots from the hot-layer vector index
nlCluster.demoWarning: This demo reuses the task-clustering MiniLM vector store and is limited to hot-layer data from the last 30 days

smartClusterSetup.title: Smart Clusters
smartClusterSetup.subtitle: Auto-archive snapshots by natural language description
smartClusterSetup.description: Smart Clusters automatically group related screenshots into buckets based on your natural-language descriptions. Powered by bge-reranker-v2-m3, delivering far better accuracy than pure vector search.
smartClusterSetup.modelInfo: A bge-reranker-v2-m3 model (~570MB, uint8 quantized) must be downloaded. This model is only used when the system is idle.
smartClusterSetup.infoNote: You can also download and enable this feature later from Settings → Feature Management.
smartClusterSetup.dismissTooltip: Permanently dismiss this prompt. Can be enabled later in settings.

smartClusters.title: Smart Clusters
smartClusters.confirmDelete: Delete this smart cluster? Assigned snapshots will not be deleted.
smartClusters.idleWarning: The background worker runs only when the system is idle.
smartClusters.noClustersHint: Create a smart cluster to auto-archive related snapshots
smartClusters.idleProcessingHint: The background worker will process pending snapshots while the system is idle
smartClusters.selectClusterToView: Select a cluster on the left to view archived snapshots

errors.not_available: Windows Hello is not available; enable it in system settings

advancedSearch.title: Advanced

search.enter_keyword: Enter a keyword or choose filters to begin.
search.press_enter: Press Enter in the top search bar to jump here quickly.
search.adjust: Adjust filters or try different keywords.

advancedSearch.nl_notice: Results obtained using natural language image search may not be accurate.
rotating: This app is currently a toy!
LLM-powered search will be added in the future!
Amiya, hear me out, I really didn't favor Pelica...

backend_shutdown.title: Backend python services shutdown
backend_shutdown.description: Please enable the backend services to continue using the application.

start.waiting: Waiting for IPC to come up...

step1.title: Install Python v{{version}}
step1.subtitle: Step 1 / 3 — choose or install system Python

pythonPath.label: Python executable path
pythonPath.placeholder: e.g.: C:\Python310\python.exe or paste custom path

venv.discovered_label: Discovered Python versions (you may select one)
venv.note: Not sure what Python is? We can auto-install it. After install the app will exit and must be restarted.

step2.title: Install required dependencies
step2.subtitle: Step 2 / 3 — create venv and install requirements and model files
step2.disk_note: This will download ~1.7GB of model files; actual time depends on network and device performance.
step2.log_start: Starting venv creation and dependency installation...

first_run.title: First-time setup required
first_run.description: The application needs to install essential environment

tasks.title: Long-term Tasks
tasks.emptyHint: Run clustering to auto-discover long-term tasks
tasks.selectHint: Select a task to view details
tasks.noData: Not enough data for clustering. Accumulate more snapshots and try again.
tasks.modelMissing: Clustering model (MiniLM-L12) not downloaded. Please download it in Settings > About.
tasks.clusteringLargeRangeReason: the clustering input contains many snapshots
tasks.clusteringLowMemoryReason: available memory is low, so full clustering may slow down or fail
tasks.clusteringDegradePrompt:
{{scope}} has about {{count}} snapshots; {{reason}}. Batched degraded mode is recommended. It clusters a sample first, then assigns the remaining snapshots in batches. Estimated peak memory is about {{estimatedGb}} GB.

Choose OK to use batched degraded mode. Choose Cancel to continue with full clustering.
tasks.clusteringDegradedNotice: Used batched degraded mode: sampled {{sampleSize}} snapshots and batch-assigned {{assignedCount}} snapshots.

securityAlert.title: Monitor integrity compromised
securityAlert.default_message: A required monitor component failed its integrity check. Startup has been blocked to protect your data.
securityAlert.recovery_hint: If you did not install or update CarbonPaper yourself, please reinstall from a trusted source. A log entry has been written to security.log under your CarbonPaper AppData directory.

ocrModelRepair.title: OCR Model Repair
ocrModelRepair.readyDescription: New screenshots will resume generating OCR text.
ocrModelRepair.description: CarbonPaper will continue saving screenshots safely, but no OCR text will be generated until the model is repaired. It will not fall back to JPEG or Python OCR.
ocrModelRepair.noAuth: Repair does not require unlocking or Windows Hello verification. Downloads are checked for size and SHA-256, and the new model loads automatically on the next OCR request.
